results.title: Nidhi Trivedi
highlights:
Data Scientist at [PACCAR](https://www.linkedin.com/company/paccar)
...
Data tells powerful stories — I build the systems, models, and insights that turn those stories into real business impact. With over 7 years of experience across data engineering/Analytics, business analytics, and data science, I specialize in transforming massive, complex datasets into scalable, insight-driven solutions. My expertise spans Python, SQL, AWS, Snowflake, ETL pipeline design, Power BI, Tableau, and ML/GenAI applications. I’ve contributed to organizations like Citi Bank, TransUnion, Paccar, and KyteData LLC, creating end-to-end data ecosystems that drive automation, efficiency, and measurable growth. I am currently a Business Analyst Intern at KyteData LLC, where I analyze business requirements, translate them into data-driven strategies, and coordinate across teams to deliver impactful analytics and reporting solutions. My work focuses on aligning data insights with business goals to improve operational efficiency and strategic decision-making. Previously at Paccar, I worked as a Data Scientist developing predictive and analytical models that enhanced fleet performance and operational reliability across large datasets. I applied advanced statistical methods and ML algorithms to identify performance trends, enabling proactive maintenance and cost reduction initiatives. At TransUnion CIBIL, I served as a Data Engineer managing large-scale consumer and commercial data pipelines across multiple countries. I designed and optimized ETL workflows and credit risk models, improving data accuracy and reducing processing time by 30%. My contributions helped enhance cross-country data integration and supported predictive model deployment for credit risk analysis. During my tenure at Citi Bank via L&T Infotech, I worked as a Data Engineer handling terabytes of financial data. I built automated, cloud-based pipelines using AWS and Snowflake, improving extraction and processing efficiency by 25%. I also developed data models and dashboards for compliance and business analytics, enabling faster, data-driven decision-making. I am passionate about designing data systems that scale, uncover insights, and accelerate business transformation. I am now seeking full-time opportunities in Data Engineering, Business Analytics, or Data Science where I can continue applying my skills to solve complex challenges and create measurable impact. Let’s connect: nidhi.s.trivedi@gmai.com
...

Competence in R, C++ • Data Visualization: Skilled in presenting complex data to drive decisions through Python plots and Tableau dashboards. I have now completed the PACCAR AI Black Belt track on DataCamp . 

The courses and projects in this track helped me master building AI/ML with Python, especially using the scikit-learn and PyTorch libraries. 

This was my 3rd out of 4 PACCAR AI belts:
...
✅ White Belt: data literacy and fundamentals
...
✅ Green Belt: data analysis
...
✅ Black Belt: data science
...
⭕ Master Black Belt: advanced data science
...
I consider our belt system to be an excellent way to spread data literacy across our organization, and I hope other organizations are doing something similar. 

My goal is to earn the Master Black Belt before the end of 2026. #ContinuousLearning #AIBlackBelt #DataScience

PACCAR is an incredible benefactor across higher education in Seattle. Having Rafael Virmond as a LLM student is such a pleasure, as he always has a kind word and an interesting story to share. #LLMdegree Seattle University School of Law Seattle University School of Law LLM Programs Meet international LLM student Rafael Virmond from Curitiba, Brazil. He spent several years working in IP and product liability related to automotive lawsuits, with Volkswagen, Audi, Nissan, and Renault among his clients. Rafael is now in-house counsel for Bellevue-based PACCAR , meaning he is currently allowed to practice law without an American law degree as long as it is limited to his role at PACCAR. He decided to pursue an LLM after his boss recommended it as a way to professionally develop himself. Graduating from Seattle University School of Law LLM Programs allows international students who have earned law degrees in other countries to sit for licensing exams in many U.S. states without having to earn a JD. Learn more about Rafael and the other international LLM students here: https://lnkd.in/gq5QjHSh

results.title: Fariba Fard
highlights:
• Nearly 3 years of experience as a Data Scientist/Data Engineer at PACCAR North Texas, with a solid foundation in statistical analysis, Machine Learning (ML), and Artificial Intelligence (AI). Proficient in building and deploying predictive ML/AI models—such as LightGBM, GBM, CatBoost, XGBoost, Random Forest, Extra Trees, k-Nearest Neighbors, Artificial Neural Networks, and Weighted Ensembles—on both textual and non-textual datasets. Skilled in presenting model outputs to end users through dashboards developed using Amazon Web Services (AWS). Experienced in prompt engineering for various Large Language Models (LLMs) to generate labels for text data, and in leveraging Generative AI to enhance productivity, particularly for code writing and debugging. Applied unsupervised ML techniques to spatial datasets using Quantum GIS (QGIS), with results visualized through interactive web maps deployed via AWS Amplify. Successfully led and supported multiple ad-hoc projects focused on data-driven insights and effective visual communication through charts and graphs. • Over 15 years of experience as a Geospatial Data Scientist at the Iranian Space Agency and the National Cartographic Center (NCC), specializing in extracting actionable insights from LiDAR point clouds, satellite imagery, aerial photographs, and topographic maps. Skilled in geospatial analytics for generating digital terrain models (DTMs), digital surface models (DSMs), orthorectified imagery, and high-resolution land use and land cover maps. • More than a decade of experience as an adjunct professor, data science instructor, and researcher, with extensive expertise in mathematics, statistics, GIS, and remote sensing.

results.title: Senior AI/ML Engineer at Jobgether - United State | Visa Sponsorship Jobs
highlights:
# Senior AI/ML Engineer
...
We are seeking a highly skilled AI/ML Engineer to develop advanced AI and ML solutions for the energy and utility sector. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making. The role requires collaboration with cross-functional teams to design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems.
...
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and deploy scalable AI and machine learning solutions integrated into production systems
...
Build reusable Python libraries and engineering components to support ML pipelines, data processing workflows, and AI system development
...
Python AWS Azure GCP Databricks LangChain LangGraph LlamaIndex Hugging Face AutoGen
...
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for an AI / ML Engineer in the United States.This role sits at the intersection of machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making, focused on solving complex challenges in the energy and utility sector. You will contribute to the development of advanced AI and ML solutions that combine predictive modeling, optimization, and generative AI to support critical infrastructure and sustainability goals. Working closely with data scientists and software engineers, you will help design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems that power real-world applications. The environment is highly collaborative and innovation-driven, with a strong emphasis on applied AI, production readiness, and measurable impact. You will work across diverse use cases including forecasting, geospatial analysis, and intelligent decision systems. This is a high-impact role where your work directly supports the modernization and efficiency of energy systems.Accountabilities
...
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and deploy scalable AI and machine learning solutions integrated into production systems.
...
- Build reusable Python libraries and engineering components to support ML pipelines, data processing workflows, and AI system development.
...
- Contribute to the design and implementation of modern AI architectures, including RAG systems, LLM integrations, and agent-based workflows.
...
- Support database design and optimization, including writing efficient queries for large-scale data processing and retrieval.
...
- Translate complex machine learning and AI requirements into structured technical tasks and actionable development plans.
...
- Stay current with advancements in machine learning engineering, generative AI, and AI orchestration, integrating best practices into workflows.
...
- Contribute to
...
- Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Software Engineering, or related field (PhD preferred).
...
- 7+ years of experience building, deploying, and maintaining machine learning systems in production environments.
...
- Strong proficiency in Python, including experience building reusable packages and scalable data pipelines.
...
- Hands-on experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP, including CI/CD and containerized deployments.
...
- Solid understanding of ML system design, MLOps principles, and end-to-end model lifecycle management.
...
- Experience with distributed computing frameworks and relational/non-relational databases.
...
- Familiarity with Databricks and large-scale data processing environments.
...
- Experience working with generative AI frameworks such as LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, Hugging Face, or AutoGen.
...
- Exposure to agentic AI systems, AI orchestration, or LLM-based decision workflows is highly desirable.
...
- Nice to have: experience in the utility, energy, or power sector and strong Databricks expertise.
